title: calycadenia multiglandulosa subsp. bicolor
intro: Calycadenia multiglandulosa subsp. bicolor, also known as the purple mariposa or purple mariposa daisy, is a rare and endangered flowering plant native to California in the United States. As a medicinal plant, it has been traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Calycadenia multiglandulosa subsp. bicolor can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, although its availability is limited due to its endangered status and strict regulations surrounding its harvesting.
